articles.title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
articles.summary: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

articles.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Florida in ‘Critical Condition’
articles.summary: A spokesman for the former mayor of New York City said Mr. Giuliani was critical but stable.

articles.title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz Leaves Shipping Firms Guessing
articles.summary: The United States vowed to help tankers navigate the perilous conditions that have kept them stranded in the Persian Gulf, but it remained unclear if companies would try to get out.

articles.title: Oil Jumps as Iran Resists Trump’s Offer to Help Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.

articles.title: Mariachi Brothers Detained by ICE Invited to Open for Kacey Musgraves After Release
articles.summary: The teenagers, who had faced deportation after a high-profile detention, took the stage on Sunday thanks to an invitation from one of country music’s biggest stars.

articles.title: The U.S. Army’s ‘Big Experiment’ in the Arctic Cold
articles.summary: How would soldiers from places like Florida, Texas and Georgia fight and persevere in temperatures as low as minus 40 degrees?

articles.title: Trump Backed Forced Treatment for Homeless People. Utah Shows the Challenges.
articles.summary: State lawmakers declined to back a Trump-inspired plan to move 1,300 homeless people to a campus on the edge of Salt Lake City, but supporters are trying to keep the plan’s spirit alive.

articles.title: The many ways Trump wants to change D.C., from buildings to statues to parks
articles.summary: Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
articles.summary: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

articles.title: Not sure what to get Mom for Mother's Day? Get ideas from our listeners
articles.summary: Mother's Day is Sunday, May 10. If you're stumped on what to get the moms in your life, get some inspiration from our audience. They share the best gifts they've ever received from their loved ones.

articles.title: Here's how medication abortion works with just one drug that's still fully available
articles.summary: A federal appeals court ruling restricts telemedicine access to the abortion drug mifepristone for the whole country, at least temporarily. But another drug that remains available is safe and effectiv

articles.title: Court Ruling Upends Abortion Access in the U.S., and Trump’s New Plan for the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Plus, the complicated legacy of Spirit Airlines.

articles.title: At the 2026 Met Gala, 'Fashion is Art.' Here's what to expect
articles.summary: On the first Monday of every May, the rich, famous and well-dressed gather in New York City for the Met Gala fundraiser. Ivy Buck offers a lookahead - covering this year's theme, where to watch, who's

articles.title: Trump administration falls behind on wildfire prevention with risky fire season ahead
articles.summary: Many of the nation's overgrown forests are at high risk of burning. Under the Trump administration, work to reduce flammable vegetation fell by more than a million acres compared to previous years.

articles.title: In many states, election-denying candidates are running to control voting
articles.summary: In 23 states, including five presidential swing states, candidates who have denied election results are running for offices that have a direct role in certifying future elections, a new report finds.

articles.title: Bill aims to stop 'claim sharks' from targeting disabled vets after NPR investigation
articles.summary: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge vets for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.

articles.title: Rescuers help Timmy, a stranded whale, return home
articles.summary: Rescuers defy warnings as the stranded "Humpback Hope," also known in German media as Timmy, is carried by barge into the North Sea, racing against time to save her.

articles.title: 2 U.S. service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
articles.summary: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

articles.title: Trump says the U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

articles.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
articles.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

articles.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
articles.summary: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Blasting beats and neon lights: inside Nairobi's 'nganya' bus culture
articles.summary: In Nairobi, the matatu isn't just a ride—it's a rolling nightclub, art gallery, and heartbeat of the city all in one.
